occasion_1967: THE MOST HOLY NAME OF JESUS
petitions: Lord, graciously hear us. Bless our sovereign N. and all the royal family. Endue them with thy Holy Spirit; enrich them with thy heavenly grace; prosper them all with happiness, and bring them to thy everlasting kingdom. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Into thy hands we commend all those who are now in their last agony. May Our Lady, Consoler of the Afflicted, and St Joseph, patron of the dying, intercede for them at this moment. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Bless all those who are united with us in Christian baptism and in thy love and service, that together we may bear witness to thy name in this place and throughout the world.

Lord, graciously hear us. Guide the minds and hearts of all our citizens that they may strive to know thy will and follow it. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Guide and prosper the younger nations of the world, and assist them in their progress towards a fuller life.

occasion_1967: THE FIRST SUNDAY AFTER EPIPHANY (THE HOLY FAMILY)
petitions: Lord, graciously hear us. Grant all nations true peace and understanding. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Have pity on all prisoners and captives, those in exile, and all who suffer persecution. Strengthen and uphold them. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Bless the families of this place, that, following the example of Nazareth, they may show forth the Christian virtues.

Lord, graciously hear us. Remove all strife and bitterness from those who are at variance, so that they may serve thee in true brotherly love. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Protect all who travel by land, sea, or air, that they may come safely to their destinations. May thy holy Angels guard them. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Bless especially the families of this place, our schools, and all who teach and learn therein, the activities in which we engage, and the enterprises we undertake.

Lord, graciously hear us. Strengthen all who are engaged in our country’s defence and keep them and us from all harm. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Grant to doctors, surgeons, and nurses, wisdom and skill, sympathy and patience; and send down thy blessing on all who labour to prevent suffering. |  Lord, graciously hear us. Inspire the faithful to show forth in their lives that they are followers of thy Son, Our Lord Jesus Christ.

BENEFACTORS Reward all our benefactors, both living and dead, with the gift of eternal happiness. | BISHOPS Sanctify all bishops of the Church. Give them the grace to be true shepherds of their flocks, especially our own Bishop N. | CHURCH AND POPE Watch over, protect, and guide thy Church, and especially our Holy Father Pope N. | CITIZENS Guide the minds and hearts of all our citizens that they may strive to know thy will and to follow it. | CIVIL STRIFE Remove all strife and bitterness from those who are at variance, so that they may serve thee in true brotherly union. | COMMUNION (FIRST) Grant that all those who receive Holy Communion today may love the Blessed Sacrament and be faithful until death. | CONFESSION Grant that all those who at this time seek forgiveness of their sins may receive thy pardon and persevere in thy grace. | CONFIRMATION Grant that those who today receive the seven-fold gifts of the Holy Spirit may always be loyal and faithful Catholics and true disciples to thee. | CROPS Send us fair weather and rain in due season, that all may enjoy the fruits of the earth. | CROSSES Give us strength to bear our crosses in union with Christ Our Lord. | DEAD Lead N. thy departed servant into the company of all the saints. | Bring to a happy resurrection thy departed servant N., who on earth has been nourished by the bread of eternal life. | Grant to all our relatives, friends, and benefactors, who have departed this life, eternal rest. | Grant to the souls of thy servants the forgiveness of all their sins, that through our prayers they may obtain the pardon they have always desired. | Be mindful of those who have died in defending their country, and all who have sacrificed themselves for our well-being. | DEAD (BISHOP OR PRIEST) May this thy departed servant N., (consecrated a bishop and ) sealed with the sacred priesthood, share in the eternal priestly offering. | DOCTORS Grant to doctors, surgeons, and nurses, wisdom and skill, sympathy and patience; and send down thy blessing on all who labour to prevent suffering. | DYING Into thy hands we commend all those who are now in their last agony. May Our Lady, Consoler of the afflicted, and St Joseph, patron of the dying, intercede for them at this moment. | ENEMIES May we be given the grace to pray according to thy will for our enemies and all who persecute us. | EXILES Have pity on all prisoners and captives, those in exile, and all who suffer persecution. Strengthen and uphold them. | FAMILY Bless especially the families of this place, that, following the example of Nazareth, they may show forth the Christian virtues. | Give peace and harmony in our homes, and a deep love of our holy religion. | FELLOW CHRISTIANS Bless all those who are united with us in Christian baptism and in thy love and service, that together we may bear witness to thy name in this place and throughout the world. | FIDELITY Inspire the faithful to show forth in their lives their following of thee. | FORCES Strengthen all who are engaged in our country’s defence and keep them and us from all harm. | GOVERNMENT Bless our Sovereign N. and all the royal family. Guide those who govern our country, that they may rule with wisdom and justice. | Guide those who govern our country that they may rule with wisdom and justice. | GUIDANCE Inspire us with the light of the Holy Spirit and teach us to know and do thy holy will. | HAPPY DEATH Grant us the grace of a happy death in peace and charity. | Bless the parents of this priest and all who have helped to lead him to the altar. | HOLY SPIRIT Guide us by the light of thy Holy Spirit and teach us to know and do thy holy will. | HUNGRY Stir our conscience to help the hungry and the poor throughout the world. | INDUSTRIAL STRIFE Remove all strife and bitterness from those who are at variance, so that they may serve thee in true brotherly union. | INDUSTRIES Prosper, we pray thee, the industries of this land and all those who work in them. | INTERIOR PEACE Grant us the peace which thou alone canst give. | JUBILEES Fill our hearts with thankfulness for all the graces bestowed upon us (especially ) and grant that we may continue to be faithful until death. | LENT Give us true sorrow for our sins and a firm purpose of amendment. | LONELY Comfort the suffering, console the lonely, protect the through the union of thy servants N. and N. the grace of a new Christian family. | Preserve the happiness of this Bride and Bridegroom through a deep understanding of the Faith. | Give to this Bride
